title: Dark Knight Court

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Cold open with Homer's signature catchphrase
jokes.punchline: The iconic frustrated grunt that opens the episode
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase that's become cultural shorthand. Perfect character integration but lacks originality by this point in the series.

jokes.text: Hollow? We paid for solid!
jokes.setup: Giant chocolate bunny is melting, revealing it's hollow
jokes.punchline: Outrage at being cheated out of solid chocolate
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about consumer expectations. Well-structured but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Chief, the company's named Bunny Hollow. I thought that was where they lived.
jokes.setup: Wiggum confused about hollow chocolate bunny
jokes.punchline: He literally thought 'Bunny Hollow' was a geographic location
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum logic - his misunderstanding is both character-specific and genuinely funny. Shows rather than tells his stupidity.

jokes.text: Look at those delightful children, Smithers. All those healthy organs, ripe for the harvesting.
jokes.setup: Burns observing children at Easter celebration
jokes.punchline: Immediately thinks of harvesting their organs
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor that's perfectly in character for Burns. The juxtaposition of 'delightful children' with organ harvesting is masterfully twisted.

jokes.text: Not here, sir. Not now.
jokes.setup: Burns talking about organ harvesting at a children's event
jokes.punchline: Smithers' matter-of-fact response implies this is normal conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Smithers response - he's not horrified, just concerned about timing and location. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Blow, you little disappointments!
jokes.punchline: Adult calling children 'little disappointments' with venom
jokes.explanation: Harsh but funny insult with great economy. The casual cruelty toward children is perfectly Simpsons.

jokes.text: His amusement is tantamount to culpability!
jokes.setup: Crowd angry about eggs in instruments, sees Bart laughing
jokes.punchline: Overly formal legal language from an angry mob
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between formal language and mob mentality. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Egg don't belong in a chicken's eye. It belongs in her pee-poo-birth-hole.
jokes.setup: Angry person trying to make a point about eggs
jokes.punchline: Crude, childish terminology for female anatomy
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that feels forced. The 'pee-poo-birth-hole' construction is more juvenile than clever.

jokes.text: It's Easter; why aren't these people at work?
jokes.punchline: Doesn't understand why people get holidays off
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - his confusion about basic human concepts like holidays. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Just don't exhale your death breath directly on them.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy showing comics to customers
jokes.punchline: Casually insulting customer while being helpful
jokes.explanation: Classic Comic Book Guy - helpful and insulting simultaneously. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: The adventures of The Good Shepherd and his sidekick, The Fleecy Kid. Bah! That's his battle cry. Baa!
jokes.punchline: Sheep sound as superhero battle cry
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on sheep/shepherd theme. The battle cry concept is absurd but logical.

jokes.text: You should be out in the fresh air, kicking dogs.
jokes.setup: Burns disapproving of Bart reading comics
jokes.punchline: Suggests animal cruelty as healthy outdoor activity
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Burns moment. The casual suggestion of animal cruelty as childhood activity is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Um, the speed of light, expressed in dollars. Just give him Faraday's Constant.
jokes.punchline: Physics constants used as pricing
jokes.explanation: Clever nerd humor using scientific constants for pricing. Shows Comic Book Guy's character while being genuinely witty.

jokes.text: You've ruined Halloween, Thanksgiving, Christmas and Easter. The legendary grand slam.
jokes.punchline: Calling it a 'grand slam' like it's a baseball achievement
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and framing device. Turning holiday destruction into a sports achievement is both funny and character-appropriate for Lisa's intelligence.

jokes.text: Now do me wearing a sombrero. Now have me sleeping under a cactus.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific Mexican stereotype requests
jokes.explanation: Nelson being demanding and specific about stereotypical imagery. Feels somewhat lazy in its reliance on cultural stereotypes.

jokes.text: I would never lie to Maggie. I did not do that prank.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining he'd lie to everyone except Maggie
jokes.punchline: Using baby as moral compass for honesty
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that reveals Bart's moral hierarchy. The sincerity toward Maggie is touching and funny.

jokes.text: Wow, he passed the Maggie test. And remember how Maggie solved Cookiegate?
jokes.punchline: Reference to previous case solved by baby
jokes.explanation: Good callback that builds on established family dynamics. The '-gate' suffix is a nice touch.

jokes.text: There's only so many kids.
jokes.setup: Discussing jury selection for youth court
jokes.punchline: Pragmatic observation about limited child population
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective observation about the reality of small-town demographics. Good deadpan delivery implied.

jokes.text: as decided in the case of Rubber v. Glue and reaffirmed by I Know You Are v. What Am I.
jokes.punchline: Childhood taunts formatted as legal cases
jokes.explanation: Brilliant legal parody that transforms playground arguments into case law. Extremely quotable and original concept with perfect execution.

jokes.text: It's so frickin' boring. Run, boy! I'll create a distraction.
jokes.setup: Homer advising Bart about court proceedings
jokes.punchline: Offering to help Bart escape rather than face justice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting - prioritizing his own boredom over teaching responsibility. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Anyone can conduct Sousa.
jokes.setup: Homer conducting the band as a distraction
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comment about classical music difficulty
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical dismissal of high culture. In character but not particularly innovative.

jokes.text: Would you kids believe your Grampa once argued in front of the Supreme Court? No. Sorry. Not a chance.
jokes.punchline: Kids immediately and bluntly rejecting the claim
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with kids' honest dismissal. The rapid-fire rejections feel natural and funny.

jokes.text: Attorney General Janet Reno? That's right. And that better not be your gum on the ground. Uh, it's not, but I'll take care of it.
jokes.setup: Flashback to Grampa meeting Janet Reno
jokes.punchline: Reno concerned about gum on ground during important legal discussion
jokes.explanation: Funny characterization of Janet Reno as petty authority figure. The contrast between legal importance and gum cleanup is well-executed.

jokes.text: why would you play chess through the mail when you can play chess through the Internet? Or preferably, not play chess at all?
jokes.punchline: Escalating to dismissing chess entirely
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that reflects Bart's impatience with intellectual pursuits. The progression from mail to internet to rejection is well-structured.

jokes.text: It's not a threat if I don't know what it means. I'll fix your wagon! Thanks. I'd like that.
jokes.punchline: Bart interpreting threat literally as helpful offer
jokes.explanation: Excellent generational humor showing how old-fashioned expressions lose their meaning. Bart's literal interpretation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Reclusive millionaire, stately mansion, damaged psyche. He's just like me.
jokes.punchline: Identifying with Bruce Wayne's character traits
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware moment where Burns recognizes his similarity to a comic book character. Shows both his insight and delusion.

jokes.text: I shall be Fruit Bat Man!
jokes.setup: Burns deciding to become a crime-fighting bat
jokes.punchline: Choosing the least intimidating bat type possible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of Batman trope. Burns choosing the most harmless bat type is perfectly in character for his delusion and frailty.

jokes.text: That's not your mother, that's your bear. Well, then, where's my stuffed mother?
jokes.punchline: Implying he has a stuffed version of his mother
jokes.explanation: Delightfully twisted moment that implies Burns has taxidermied his mother. Perfect dark humor with excellent economy.

jokes.text: First, I would like to say, rook to E-8. Checkmate. Dagnabbit!
jokes.punchline: Uses opportunity to make chess move against Grandpa
jokes.explanation: Great callback - uses formal court setting to continue personal chess game

jokes.text: You're wearing sneakers to a trial? Oh, God, the press is gonna have a field day with this. Yay, field day!
jokes.setup: Janet Reno concerned about courtroom propriety
jokes.punchline: Ralph excited by literal interpretation of 'field day'
jokes.explanation: Classic Ralph moment with literal interpretation. Janet Reno's media awareness contrasts well with Ralph's innocence.

jokes.text: That's exactly what George W. Bush said when he was inaugurated. People don't remember that.
jokes.punchline: Comparing to forgotten Bush inauguration moment
jokes.explanation: Political reference that feels somewhat dated and forced. The connection between Ralph and Bush is tenuous.

jokes.text: Look, I'll give you each $1,000 if you pretend to let that old man out there stop this crime.
jokes.setup: Burns trying to be a superhero
jokes.punchline: Bribing criminals to let him 'stop' them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment - solving problems with money even when playing hero

jokes.text: I am the squeak in the rafters, the flutter in the chimney, the tiny teeth marks in the peach.
jokes.punchline: All his threats are tiny and adorable
jokes.explanation: Masterful parody of Batman's intimidating introductions, perfectly scaled down to Burns' frailty. Each element is perfectly chosen to be non-threatening.

jokes.text: Feel the crumbling fist of justice! Ouchers. Oh, dear. Who are you, mysterious octogenarian of the night?
jokes.punchline: Weak 'crumbling fist' and being called 'octogenarian of the night'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of superhero tropes - 'crumbling fist' and 'octogenarian of the night'

jokes.text: You have to keep squeezing this thing? Who has the strength for that?
jokes.setup: Burns trying to use a keychain penlight
jokes.punchline: Complaining about minimal physical effort required
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment highlighting his extreme frailty. The complaint about such minimal effort is both pathetic and funny.

jokes.text: Him? All he takes are sodas and desserts.
jokes.setup: Asked if Bart could steal eggs
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comment about his selective stealing
jokes.explanation: Good character insight showing Bart's predictable theft patterns. Shows how well the staff knows him.

jokes.text: No one could have touched those eggs except me and the seagulls that laid them.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of seagulls as egg source
jokes.explanation: Casually absurd detail about school food sourcing. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: who certainly isn't me. Smithers, wink my eye.
jokes.setup: Burns trying to be secretive about his identity
jokes.punchline: Too frail to wink on his own
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns physical comedy. The image of needing assistance to wink is both pathetic and hilarious.

jokes.text: Want to hit the hot tub, Burnsie? Well, I hate to get all pruney, but heated tubbery is what we playboys do.
jokes.punchline: Burns using 'heated tubbery' as playboy activity
jokes.explanation: Burns' delusions of being a playboy with made-up terminology

jokes.text: Yeah, but I'd pee my pants. Nelson. Dry as a bone.
jokes.setup: Milhouse would lie and wet himself from nerves
jokes.punchline: Nelson checking proves Milhouse isn't lying
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor using Milhouse's nervousness as a lie detector. Nelson's matter-of-fact checking is funny.

jokes.text: Now, that's what I call 'legal briefs.' I thought of the joke and rushed over.
jokes.punchline: Underwear/legal document pun with self-aware delivery
jokes.explanation: Obvious pun delivered with self-awareness that doesn't redeem its predictability. Skinner's eagerness makes it slightly endearing.

jokes.text: The prosecution calls everyone in the world!
jokes.setup: Opening door to character witnesses against Bart
jokes.punchline: Literally calling the entire world population
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how universally disliked Bart is. The hyperbolic response is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: He asked for, um... ...Mike Rotch. And then what did you say? Well, I said, um... I said, 'Mike Rotch.' Um, 'Mike Rotch.'
jokes.setup: Moe testifying about Bart's prank calls
jokes.punchline: Having to repeatedly say the crude name in court
jokes.explanation: Good callback to classic gag with the added cringe of formal testimony. Moe's discomfort makes it work.

jokes.text: I just had to make sure that he never did this to another bartender.
jokes.punchline: Treating prank calls as serious public service
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moe's wounded dignity. His serious treatment of prank calls is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Pass the gravy, Gloria All-Wrong. The trial's not over, and gravy is dead animal juice.
jokes.setup: Homer making light of the situation at dinner
jokes.punchline: Lisa's correction about both the trial and gravy
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast with Homer's dismissiveness and Lisa's precise corrections. The name-calling feels natural.

jokes.text: Just remember this: a Simpson never gives up. You got to be kidding!
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately contradicts it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer deflation of inspirational moment

jokes.text: a man who claims to be a hero but is nothing more than a fraud with a cute little bod.
jokes.setup: Smithers calling out Burns' fake heroism
jokes.punchline: Ending with compliment about his body
jokes.explanation: Perfect Smithers moment combining criticism with inappropriate attraction. The shift from harsh truth to flirtation is expertly done.

jokes.text: And so I've learned that the only true Batmen are Christian Bale and Adam West.
jokes.punchline: References real Batman actors
jokes.explanation: Meta reference to Batman actors - breaks fourth wall slightly

jokes.text: This skirt has a small splatter. Splatter as if the egg had been crushed against it by hand. Like someone faked being hit, so they wouldn't be a suspect. It's not a skirt. It's a kilt!
jokes.punchline: Willie's indignant correction about his traditional garment
jokes.explanation: Good mystery resolution with Willie's predictable offense about his kilt. The detective work is solid and the reaction in character.

jokes.text: Because I hate Easter. You see, ladies, I'm not just a Presbyterian, I'm a Scottish Old Believer Presbyterian!
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific religious denomination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie moment with escalating religious specificity. The denominational detail feels authentic to his character.

jokes.text: I made sweet love to Lunchlady Dora, then copied the key as she slept.
jokes.setup: How Willie got access to the eggs
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortably graphic confession about seduction
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable revelation that fits Willie's crude character. The spy movie trope applied to lunch lady seduction is funny.

jokes.text: You're so light. It's like fighting a silk scarf! Or a kimono sash!
jokes.punchline: Comparing his weight to delicate fabric items
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy highlighting Burns' frailty. The specific fabric comparisons add color to the insult.

jokes.text: Yes, feel the weightless tickle of justice.
jokes.setup: Burns trying to be intimidating in fight
jokes.punchline: Describing his punches as gentle tickling
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns moment where he tries to sound threatening while acknowledging his weakness. The phrase 'weightless tickle of justice' is poetry.

jokes.text: Which side won? Good or evil? Good. And which side was I on? Also good.
jokes.setup: Burns confused about the fight outcome
jokes.punchline: Surprised to learn he was on the good side
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment showing his self-awareness about usually being evil. The surprise at being good is genuinely funny.

jokes.text: We have a school full of professionals, sir. Really? Where is that school? Inside your head?
jokes.punchline: Burns' dismissive insult about imaginary competence
jokes.explanation: Good Burns insult that's both mean-spirited and creative. Shows his dismissive attitude toward public institutions.

jokes.text: You saved me, Lis. For the rest of my life, you'll always be my one phone call. Aw, but I do hope you'll try to be good from now on. Don't need to; I got you.
jokes.setup: Touching sibling moment about Lisa saving Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately plans to rely on her for future crimes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - learning wrong lesson from sister's help

jokes.text: Don't need to; I got you.
jokes.setup: Lisa hoping Bart will be good
jokes.punchline: Bart admitting he'll rely on Lisa to bail him out
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling dynamic captured in few words. Shows Bart's dependence on Lisa while being both sweet and infuriating.

jokes.text: Maybe I should wrap them around your gabby little throat. Ah! Tenting, tenting! Back to the tenting!
jokes.punchline: Burns threatening her then retreating to safer pose
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and retreat showing Burns' evil nature balanced with his fear of Lisa. The 'tenting' callback is effective.

jokes.text: Acquitted? Then my nightmare isn't over!
jokes.punchline: Someone's dismay that Bart is still free
jokes.explanation: Community reaction to Bart remaining free to cause more chaos

jokes.text: Gentlemen, this threat is too great for just one elderly hero. We have no choice but to activate The Octogenarian Initiative.
jokes.punchline: Assembling team of elderly superheroes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Avengers parody with geriatric heroes - 'Octogenarian Initiative'

jokes.text: Gentlemen, this threat is too great for just one elderly hero. We have no choice but to activate The Octogenarian Initiative.
jokes.setup: Burns facing the Abominable Dr. Lenny
jokes.punchline: Avengers parody with elderly superheroes
jokes.explanation: Excellent superhero parody adapted for elderly characters. The name 'Octogenarian Initiative' is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: The Rambler... And then there was the time I taught a dog to make oatmeal.
jokes.punchline: Immediately going off on tangent story
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa moment - can't stay focused even during superhero introduction. The dog/oatmeal detail is wonderfully random.

jokes.text: I open one, no trump. Double. You've just made a powerful enemy.
jokes.punchline: They're playing bridge instead of fighting crime
jokes.explanation: Perfect anti-climax - elderly superheroes immediately default to bridge game

tmdb_overview: Springfield's Easter is ruined by a prank and all fingers point to Bart. It is up to Lisa to save her brother, in a student court presided over by Attorney General Janet Reno. Meanwhile, inspired by an old comic book, Mr. Burns becomes an ineffectual superhero—fruit bat man.
